response:
<<Geometry of Community Design>>

The hexagonal community center has six equal sides of 30 meters. A regular hexagon can be divided into six equilateral triangles. Each triangle has a side length of 30 meters, so the area of one triangle is calculated using the formula $ \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times \text{side}^2 $. The total area of the hexagon is six times the area of one triangle, giving $ 6 \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times 30^2 $. The area of the hexagon is approximately 2,338.27 square meters.

* * *

The circular garden touches the midpoints of the hexagon's sides, making its diameter equal to the distance between two opposite sides. This distance is twice the apothem of the hexagon. The apothem can be found using the formula $ \frac{\text{side}}{2 \times \tan(\frac{\pi}{6})} $. The apothem is 25.98 meters, so the diameter of the garden is 51.96 meters. The radius of the garden is half of this, approximately 25.98 meters. The area of the circular garden is calculated using $ \pi \times \text{radius}^2 $, resulting in approximately 2,122.15 square meters. The committee [address] will oversee the final layout of the garden and community center.
